why_it_matters: Single-instance vocalization is common and often context-driven
immediate_action: Increase distance slightly and reward calm behavior
long_term_support: Gradually reintroduce similar environments with reinforcement
escalate_when: If barking becomes repeated or spreads to other environments

why_it_matters: Repeated vocalization reduces focus and may generalize
immediate_action: Reduce stimulation and reward calm check-ins early
long_term_support: Build tolerance through gradual exposure and reinforcement
escalate_when: If barking increases or occurs across multiple settings

why_it_matters: Overarousal can interfere with learning and responsiveness
immediate_action: Pause and re-engage with simple cues
long_term_support: Shorten sessions and reinforce focus before increasing difficulty
escalate_when: If behavior worsens or affects responsiveness to commands

why_it_matters: Initial fear responses are common in new environments
escalate_when: If fear persists or spreads to familiar environments

why_it_matters: Repeated fear responses can generalize and become harder to reverse
immediate_action: Reduce exposure intensity and avoid overwhelming situations
escalate_when: If fear increases or impacts daily functioning

why_it_matters: Growling signals discomfort and should be respected
immediate_action: Create distance and avoid forcing interaction
long_term_support: Build positive associations with controlled exposure
escalate_when: If growling becomes more frequent or escalates

immediate_action: Run short focus exercises before adding distraction
long_term_support: Build engagement gradually in more complex settings
escalate_when: If engagement does not improve with easier training

immediate_action: Simplify commands and reward quick responses
long_term_support: Increase consistency and reduce competing stimuli
escalate_when: If responsiveness declines or fails to improve

why_it_matters: Impulsivity can interfere with service dog behavior standards
immediate_action: Wait for calm before rewarding attention
escalate_when: If behavior escalates or spreads across contexts

why_it_matters: Excitable greetings are common but should decrease over time
long_term_support: Gradually increase exposure to new people
escalate_when: If excitement increases or becomes disruptive

why_it_matters: Jumping is common but should reduce with consistent reinforcement
immediate_action: Ignore jumping and reward calm posture

why_it_matters: High distraction suggests training difficulty is too high
escalate_when: If distraction worsens or affects learning

why_it_matters: Avoidance can indicate discomfort with unfamiliar stimuli
immediate_action: Allow exploration at a safe distance
long_term_support: Use gradual exposure with positive reinforcement
escalate_when: If avoidance becomes consistent or spreads

why_it_matters: Mouthing often reflects excitement or lack of regulation
escalate_when: If mouthing escalates or becomes aggressive

why_it_matters: Positive demeanor is a protective signal for training success
escalate_when: If positive behavior declines over time

why_it_matters: Positive social behavior is associated with successful outcomes
escalate_when: If play becomes inappropriate or escalates

why_it_matters: Tolerance to handling predicts training success
Confidence in environments supports training progression
